title: Python 3.14, pygame-ce 2.5.7, SDL, Initialization, and the Main Loop · Program Brief: Quit Cleanup
summary: A script imports an incompatible pygame distribution, opens a window during import, ignores failed modules and quit events, presents twice, and never cleans up. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the quit cleanup increment in owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…
objectives: Handle window and program exit through one owned stop path that releases resources and returns a status.
Order event intake, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, presentation, and timing once per frame.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-runtime-sdl-loop-4
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-runtime-sdl-loop-4.
# Case 1655: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def worked_asteroids_runtime_sdl_loop_4(python_minor: int = 14, pygame_ce_minor: int = 7, initialized_modules: int = 5, quit_handled: bool = True, cleanup_called: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "playerworked4",
        "seed1655",
        "input4dekrow",
        "frameworked41655",
        "state1655worked4",
        "playtest4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if python_minor < 14 or pygame_ce_minor < 7:
        return False, python_minor, "unsupported-runtime"
    if initialized_modules < 3:
        return False, initialized_modules, "partial-init"
    if not quit_handled:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-quit"
    if not cleanup_called:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-cleanup"
    assert initialized_modules >= 3
    return True, initialized_modules, "owned-runtime"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

# Evidence: asteroids-quit-cleanup-1e8jpo
# Competency: asteroids-quit-cleanup.
# Case 2164: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_quit_cleanup_1e8jpo(python_minor: int = 14, pygame_ce_minor: int = 7, initialized_modules: int = 5, quit_handled: bool = True, cleanup_called: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1e8jpo = "1e8jpo-opj8e1-2164"
    assert evidence_variant_1e8jpo
    evidence_axes_1e8jpo = (
        "player1e8jpo",
        "seed2164",
        "inputopj8e1",
        "frame1e8jpo2164",
        "state21641e8jpo",
        "playtestopj8e12164",

    if python_minor < 14 or pygame_ce_minor < 7:
        return False, python_minor, "unsupported-runtime"
    if initialized_modules < 3:
        return False, initialized_modules, "partial-init"
    if not quit_handled:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-quit"
    if not cleanup_called:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-cleanup"
    assert initialized_modules >= 3
    return True, initialized_modules, "owned-runtime"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Quit Cleanup model · Theory Quit Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Quit Cleanup: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 5881 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle window and program exit through one owned stop path that releases resources and returns a status.
